title: phlegmariurus coralium
intro: Phlegmariurus coralium, also known as the Coral Club Moss, is a type of ancient vascular plant that belongs to the lycopod family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Phlegmariurus coralium are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of Phlegmariurus coralium are typically made from its leaves and stems, and are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal disorders.
